fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Florida Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity florida, how to transfer physical therapist license to florida, physical therapist compact florida, florida physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Florida Board of Physical Therapy Practice
board.parent_dept: Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine
board.mailing_address:
Florida Board of Physical Therapy
4052 Bald Cypress Way, Bin C05
Tallahassee, FL 32399-3255

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Florida.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Visit the MQA Online Services Portal (https://mqa-internet.doh.state.fl.us/MQAServices/Default.aspx?Agency=PHY) and create a new account if you are a first-time applicant. You will set up a user ID and password.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your MQA Online Services Portal account, select 'Apply for a new license', and choose 'Physical Therapist' from the available professions. Complete all sections of the application thoroughly.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program to be sent directly to the Florida Board of Physical Therapy. Also, complete the required background screening via LiveScan fingerprinting as instructed on the portal.

steps.title: Pass Required Examinations
steps.description: Ensure your passing scores for the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) are sent directly to the Florida Board from the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT). You must also pass the Florida Laws and Rules Examination, which may be administered online or through a testing vendor.

steps.title: Pay Application and Licensure Fees
steps.description: Submit the required application fee and, upon approval, the initial licensure fee through the MQA Online Services Portal. Total fees for licensure by endorsement typically include both.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Florida physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $175. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Florida have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Florida participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Florida without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Florida?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Florida
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Florida, you must submit an online application through the Florida Department of Health MQA Online Services Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the MQA Online Services Portal, select 'Register' or 'Create a New Account' if you are a first-time user, follow the prompts to create your user ID and password, then log in to start your application.
